---
description: Security rules for the DevOpsWes blog repository
applyTo: "**"
---

# Security Rules

## Secrets & Credentials

- **Never** commit secrets, passwords, API keys, tokens, or private keys to the repository.
- This is a public GitHub Pages repository. Anything committed is permanently public.
- Environment-specific config (if ever needed) goes in `.env` files which must be gitignored.

## Authentication

- Use SSH key authentication only. Never suggest or configure password-based auth.
- GitHub Actions (if ever added) must use short-lived GITHUB_TOKEN or OIDC, not long-lived PATs stored as secrets.

## Dependencies

- `Gemfile` uses `gem "github-pages"` only. This pins all Jekyll dependencies to the GitHub Pages-approved bundle — this is intentional and must not be changed.
- Do not add `gem "jekyll"` directly alongside `gem "github-pages"`.
- Do not introduce npm, Node.js build tooling, or other package managers unless explicitly requested. This site builds entirely via Jekyll on GitHub Pages with zero client-side build steps.

## Content Security

- All user-facing content is static HTML generated by Jekyll. There is no server-side code, no database, no form handling.
- Do not add JavaScript that loads remote scripts from untrusted CDNs without a Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash.
- Google Fonts is loaded from `fonts.googleapis.com` — this is acceptable and intentional.

## Domain & Hosting

- Custom domain: `www.devopswes.com` (configured in `CNAME` file and GitHub Pages settings).
- Do not modify the `CNAME` file.
- HTTPS is enforced by GitHub Pages — do not add or suggest any HTTP redirect workarounds.
